description:
A chemical storage facility has recovered 5 unlabeled bottles (A, B, C, D, E) and 5 loose labels after a flood. Identify the chemical in each bottle by comparing the lab measurements provided in `~/Desktop/lab_measurements.txt` against official physical property data (Boiling Point, Flash Point, Solubility) from CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/) for the following candidates: Acetonitrile, Cyclohexane, Tetrahydrofuran, Pyridine, and Ethyl Acetate. 

Create a forensic identification report at `~/Documents/chemical_identification_report.txt` using the exact format below, including the reference Boiling Point (BP) and Flash Point (FP) values found in CAMEO to justify each match:

FORENSIC IDENTIFICATION REPORT
==============================
Bottle A: [Chemical Name] (Ref BP: [Value], Ref FP: [Value])
Bottle B: [Chemical Name] (Ref BP: [Value], Ref FP: [Value])
Bottle C: [Chemical Name] (Ref BP: [Value], Ref FP: [Value])
Bottle D: [Chemical Name] (Ref BP: [Value], Ref FP: [Value])
Bottle E: [Chemical Name] (Ref BP: [Value], Ref FP: [Value])
